tasks.description: Add MCP Integration section to website homepage
tasks.context:
The Invoica MCP server shipped (backend/src/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.ts). Developers need to discover it from invoica.ai.

CREATE: website/components/McpIntegration.tsx

DESIGN: Dark section (bg-invoica-blue), same max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 wrapper as existing components. No 'use client' needed.

HTML STRUCTURE:
- Section id='mcp' className='py-28 bg-invoica-blue relative'
- Top badge chip: inline-flex pill reading 'MCP Server' in invoica-purple accent style
- H2: 'Use Invoica from Claude, Cursor & Windsurf' — text-white, text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight
- Subtext: 'Your AI assistant can create invoices, list payments, and verify on-chain settlements — directly from the chat.' — text-invoica-gray-400 text-lg
- Two code blocks side by side on md+, stacked on mobile:
  LEFT: label 'Install (one command)' + block with: npx -y @invoica/mcp
  RIGHT: label 'Claude Desktop config' + block with the JSON snippet (mcpServers > invoica > command/args)
- Three tool pills in a flex row (wrap on mobile):
  create_invoice, list_invoices, check_settlement
  Style: inline-flex items-center px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-white/5 text-invoica-gray-300 border border-white/10
- CTA row: 'Get your free API key' link to https://app.invoica.ai/api-keys?utm_source=website&utm_medium=mcp&utm_campaign=beta2026
  Style: text-invoica-purple-light hover:text-white underline text-sm

CODE BLOCKS: use a div with bg-black/40 border border-white/10 rounded-xl p-4 font-mono text-sm text-green-400

AFTER creating the component, ALSO edit website/app/page.tsx:
1. Add import McpIntegration from '@/components/McpIntegration'; after the CodeExample import
2. Add <McpIntegration /> in the JSX right after <CodeExample /> and before <Enterprise />

Keep the component under 120 lines. Match the existing color class names exactly (invoica-blue, invoica-purple, invoica-gray-400, etc.) — check Footer.tsx and Pricing.tsx for reference color usage.

tasks.description: Gas Backstop module — ETH balance checker (Priority 1 product feature per SOUL.md)
Welcome email on API key creation — onboarding retention for beta
tasks.context: Every new API key creation should trigger a welcome email. This is critical during the 60-day free beta to drive activation. CREATE: backend/src/services/email/welcome-email.ts Use nodemailer (already in package.json). Env vars: SMTP_HOST (default smtp.gmail.com), SMTP_PORT (default 587), SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, EMAIL_FROM (default hello@invoica.ai). If SMTP_USER is not set, log 'SMTP not configured — skipping welcome email' and return. Do not throw. Export one function: export async function sendWelcomeEmail(to: string, apiKeyPrefix: string): Promise The email: Subject: 'Welcome to Invoica — your API key is ready' HTML body with inline styles (email clients strip external CSS): - h2: 'Welcome to Invoica' style='font-family:sans-serif;color:#0A2540' - p: 'Your key starts with: apiKeyPrefix... Keep it secret.' - p: '3 things to do next:' - ol with li items: 1. Read the 10-minute quickstart 2. Create your first invoice via API 3. Follow @invoica_ai for updates - p style='color:#6B7280;font-size:13px': 'Questions? Reply here or email support@invoica.ai' text: Plain text version of same content AFTER creating the file, ALSO edit backend/src/api/api-keys.ts: - Add at top: import { sendWelcomeEmail } from '../services/email/welcome-email'; - Inside createApiKeyHandler, find: res.status(201).json(result); - Add the fire-and-forget call BEFORE that line: sendWelcomeEmail(parseResult.data.customerEmail ?? '', (result.key ?? '').slice(0, 8)).catch(() => {}); Check what fields exist on parseResult.data and result by reading the top of api-keys.ts and the createApiKeySchema. Use the correct field name for customerEmail — it might be email or customerEmail. Check createApiKeySchema in validators/api-key-schemas.ts if needed. Max 80 lines for the email service. Minimal edit to api-keys.ts.

tasks.description: npm publish pipeline for @invoica/mcp — GitHub Actions + mcp-package scaffold
tasks.context:
NPM_TOKEN is already set as a GitHub Actions secret. The @invoica npm org exists. We need to wire up the publish pipeline so `npx @invoica/mcp` works after the first tag push.

The root package.json is private — we cannot publish from it. Solution: create a standalone mcp-package/ directory that IS the npm package, compiled from the TypeScript source.

CREATE THESE 4 FILES:

1. tsconfig.mcp.json (root level) — narrow compile config for just the MCP server:
{
  "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "./dist/mcp",
    "rootDir": "./backend/src/mcp",
    "declaration": false,
    "declarationMap": false,
    "sourceMap": false
  },
  "include": ["backend/src/mcp/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}

2. mcp-package/package.json — the publishable npm package:
{
  "name": "@invoica/mcp",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Invoica MCP Server — create invoices, list payments, and check on-chain settlements from Claude, Cursor & Windsurf",
  "bin": { "invoica-mcp": "./index.js" },
  "files": ["index.js", "README.md"],
  "engines": { "node": ">=18.0.0" },
  "dependencies": { "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.0.0" },
  "keywords": ["mcp", "invoica", "x402", "ai-agents", "payments", "invoicing"],
  "license": "MIT"
}

3. mcp-package/README.md — npm README (copy the content from backend/src/mcp/README.md exactly — read that file and reproduce it here).

4. .github/workflows/publish-mcp.yml — GitHub Actions publish workflow:

name: Publish @invoica/mcp

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'npm-v*'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Compile MCP server
        run: npx tsc -p tsconfig.mcp.json
      - name: Build mcp-package/index.js (add shebang + compiled output)
        run: |
          printf '#!/usr/bin/env node\n' > mcp-package/index.js
          cat dist/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.js >> mcp-package/index.js
          chmod +x mcp-package/index.js
      - name: Publish to npm
        working-directory: mcp-package
        run: npm publish --access public
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

That is everything. Max 25 lines per file. After this ships, the publish command is:
  git tag npm-v1.0.0 && git push origin npm-v1.0.0

Do NOT create mcp-package/index.js — the workflow generates it at publish time from the compiled TypeScript. Only create the 4 files listed above.

tasks.description: Draft X thread — Invoica MCP Server launch announcement
tasks.context:
The Invoica MCP server shipped in commit 8380cdf. @invoica_ai needs an X/Twitter thread announcing it.

CREATE: reports/cmo/x-thread-mcp-launch.md

FORMAT: 5-tweet thread. Number each tweet [1/5] through [5/5]. Each tweet must be UNDER 280 characters including the thread marker.

TONE: Technical, confident. Written for Claude Code / Cursor / Windsurf developers. No hype words. Facts only.

CONTENT:
[1/5] Hook — announce that Invoica now has an MCP server. What problem it solves in one line (no API docs needed, AI assistant does the billing).
[2/5] The install. Just: npx -y @invoica/mcp — then one line what it unlocks. Then: 'Claude Desktop config: { mcpServers: { invoica: { command: npx, args: [-y, @invoica/mcp] } } }'
[3/5] The 3 tools: create_invoice — list_invoices — check_settlement. One sentence each.
[4/5] The x402 angle: settlements are verified on-chain (Base), payment proofs include txHash. No subscription — pay per invoice.
[5/5] CTA: free API key at app.invoica.ai/api-keys — free during beta — @NexusCollectv

No hashtags. Use line breaks for readability. Output the markdown file with this exact header:
# @invoica_ai X Thread — MCP Server Launch

Draft for review and approval before scheduling.

Max 35 lines total.
